ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for coffee-flavoured Mexican-style filled bun; closest parent 'bread product' (FOODON_03302050) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E 'Bread and similar products' is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for coffee-filled sweet bun exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 'Bread roll' is the closest parent concept; no specific code for coffee Mexican bun exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Coffee Mexican Bun typically weighs approximately 90 g and constitutes a single serving as sold at a bakery or café.

nutrient_source.title: Sweet bread roll with filling — composite estimate based on USDA FoodData Central entries for enriched sweet bread (FDC ID 325871) and butter-based pastry components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Coffee Mexican Bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy entries for sweet enriched bread rolls (FDC ID 325871, 'Bread, sweet, enriched') and butter-based pastry dough components, adjusted for the coffee-custard filling and crispy coffee glaze. The bun is a sponge-dough butter bread with coffee custard filling and coffee-flavoured crispy topping, analogous to a high-fat, high-sugar sweet bread roll. Macros are consistent with published values for similar Chinese bakery buns (Rotiboy-style) reported in Malaysian and Singaporean food composition literature.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread and enriched sweet bread products — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entry for white bread (GI = 70, glucose reference)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Coffee Mexican Bun. GI estimated at 70 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white bread (GI = 70, glucose reference scale), which is the closest defensible analogue for a refined-flour, butter-enriched sweet bun. The high sugar content (~18.5 g/100 g) and refined flour base support a high GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (70 × 46.8 g) / 100 ≈ 32.8, where available carbohydrate per 90 g serving = 52.0 × 0.90 = 46.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~279 mg per 90 g serving) and high saturated fat from butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Coffee Mexican Bun consumption to occasional treats, as each bun contributes approximately 279 mg sodium and significant saturated fat, both of which can adversely affect blood pressure and vascular tone when consumed regularly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) and high sugar content (~16.7 g per 90 g serving) causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Coffee Mexican Bun, as its high GI (approximately 70) and high glycemic load (approximately 32.8 per bun) will cause a rapid and substantial rise in postprandial blood glucose, making glycaemic management significantly more difficult.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~7.4 g per 90 g serving) and trans fat (~0.18 g per 90 g serving) from butter and pastry glaze
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat Coffee Mexican Bun as an occasional indulgence only, given its high saturated fat content from butter and the presence of trans fat in the crispy glaze, both of which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coffee Mexican Bun (咖啡墨西哥包) is a popular Chinese-bakery sweet bread originating from the Malaysian and Singaporean Chinese bakery tradition, closely associated with the Rotiboy style of coffee bun. It consists of a soft, moist sponge-dough butter bread roll filled with a specially blended coffee custard, delivering a rich, aromatic coffee flavour with a creamy interior. The bun is topped with a distinctive crispy coffee-flavoured Mexican glaze — a piped cookie-dough crust made from butter, sugar, egg, and coffee powder — which bakes into a crunchy, fragrant shell that contrasts with the pillowy interior. Key ingredients include wheat flour, butter, sugar, eggs, coffee powder, cocoa powder, cinnamon powder, and salt. Nutritionally, one bun (approximately 90 g) provides around 331 kcal, 14.5 g fat (of which 8.2 g is saturated), 52 g carbohydrate, and 18.5 g sugar per 100 g, placing it in the high-energy, high-sugar, high-saturated-fat category. Its glycemic index is estimated at approximately 70 (high), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) is moderate and relevant for hypertension management. Clinically, this bun should be treated as an occasional treat for individuals with cardiometabolic conditions. It is vegetarian but not vegan, not gluten-free, and not dairy-free. In Singapore, it is commonly sold at Chinese bakeries, dessert shops, and food delivery platforms such as field survey.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Coffee Mexican Bun was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for enriched sweet bread rolls and butter-based pastry components, adjusted for the coffee-custard filling and crispy glaze. GI is estimated by analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white bread (GI = 70). Confidence is medium because the estimation basis is defensible and consistent with published values for similar Chinese bakery buns, but no direct measurement exists.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for enriched sweet bread (FDC ID 325871) and butter-based pastry components; no exact match exists in any approved primary source for this specific product. 2. GI is estimated at 70 by analogy with white bread (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for Coffee Mexican Bun was found in the peer-reviewed literature. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; this is confirmed zero as sweet bread rolls contain negligible ascorbic acid and USDA SR Legacy reports 0 mg for this food category. 7. The food_group in the input JSON was listed as 'Beverages', which is incorrect; this record has been reclassified to 'Snacks' as the Coffee Mexican Bun is a solid baked bread product. 8. Halal status is listed as false as it cannot be confirmed without restaurant-level certification; the product contains butter and eggs which are permissible, but certification status for the specific vendor (RUXU 入续) is unknown. 9. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (70 × 46.8) / 100 = 32.8, where available carbohydrate per 90 g serving = 52.0 × 0.90 = 46.8 g.
